---
title: "CESM Ocean Temperature Toolkit"
author: "Natalia Jorbenadze"
format: revealjs
---

# Motivation

::: {.incremental}
- CESM ocean model output is powerful but difficult to work with directly
- Data are large, cloud-hosted, and not immediately analysis-ready
- This project builds a lightweight Python toolkit for ocean temperature diagnostics
:::

---

# Problem

How can we move from raw CESM ocean temperature output to interpretable climate diagnostics?

::: {.incremental}
- Surface ocean temperature
- Climatology
- Anomalies
- Variability time series
:::

---

# Package Goal

`enso_toolkit` simplifies a basic climate-analysis workflow:

```python
da = open_cesm_temp_surface()
anom = compute_global_mean_anomaly(da)
```

::: {.incremental}
- Reduces repeated code
- Makes the workflow reusable
- Turns raw data into diagnostics
:::

---

# Package Structure

```text
enso_final_project/
├── enso_toolkit/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   └── core.py
├── examples/
│   └── quickstart.py
├── README.md
└── pyproject.toml
```

---

# Core Functions

::: {.incremental}
- `open_cesm_temp_surface()`
- `summarize_dataarray()`
- `plot_first_timestep()`
- `compute_spatial_mean()`
- `compute_monthly_climatology()`
- `compute_monthly_anomalies()`
- `compute_global_mean_anomaly()`
- `compute_variance()`
:::

---

# Key Function: Data Access

```python
def open_cesm_temp_surface():
    da = open_cesm2le(
        "TEMP",
        component="ocn",
        scenario="historical",
        forcing="cmip6",
        time_slice=("1990-01", "1991-12"),
        members=0,
    )
    return da.isel(z_t=0)
```

::: {.incremental}
- Opens CESM ocean temperature
- Extracts the surface layer
- Returns an analysis-ready `xarray.DataArray`
:::

---

# Key Function: Anomaly Time Series

```python
spatial_mean = da.mean(dim=["nlat", "nlon"]).load()
climatology = spatial_mean.groupby("time.month").mean("time")
global_anom = spatial_mean.groupby("time.month") - climatology
```

::: {.incremental}
- Computes spatial mean first for speed
- Removes the monthly climatology
- Produces a temperature anomaly time series
:::

---

# Result 1: Surface Temperature

![Surface ocean temperature](your_map.png)

---

# Result 2: Anomaly Time Series

![Temperature anomaly time series](your_timeseries.png)

---

# Variability Metric

```python
variance = compute_variance(global_anom)
```

::: {.incremental}
- Variance summarizes the strength of variability
- Adds a quantitative diagnostic beyond plotting
- Useful for comparing longer runs later
:::

---

# Design Tradeoffs

::: {.incremental}
- Used a short time window for fast prototyping
- Computed spatial mean before climatology to reduce compute time
- Focused on a reliable pipeline instead of many unfinished features
- Niño 3.4 masking is future work due to CESM grid complexity
:::

---

# Reflection

If I started again, I would:

::: {.incremental}
- Set up the package structure earlier
- Separate plotting from analysis functions
- Improve ocean-grid handling
- Extend to longer time periods
:::

---

# Future Work

::: {.incremental}
- Implement Niño 3.4 index
- Extend time window
- Add ensemble members
- Compare with CAMulator
- Improve visualizations
:::

---

# Conclusion

::: {.incremental}
- Built an installable Python package for CESM ocean analysis
- Implemented climatology, anomaly, and variability workflows
- Generated interpretable climate diagnostics
- Established a foundation for ENSO analysis
:::
